Violet Lynch, 9, and Trinity Burkheart, 8, work on a papier-mache prop during Thursday’s summer workshop at the Joplin Little Theatre. This week, the theater will perform “Ants Working Together” at noon and 5 p.m. Friday. The performances are open to the public.Globe | Roger Nomer
Christian Lei-Keil, 9, and Ethan Landers, 10, work on their chalk drawing of a school during a summer art camp Tuesday at Spiva Center for the Arts.Globe | Roger Nomer

Eric Haun shows his love for Joplin on Saturday at his booth at the Empire Market. Haun has painted 19 Joplin hearts on buildings throughout the city and has more planned. On Saturday, Haun handed out maps and stickers, and he sold T-shirts, large maps, buttons and more. Globe | Laurie Sisk
Vinita, Okla., resident Ian Mariott eyes the basket while playing disc golf Saturday at Landreth Park. Mariott said he likes coming to Joplin to play its courses. Globe | Laurie Sisk
Bella Vista native and lead singer for Big Smitty, Shawn Smith, entertains the crowd during Food Truck Friday at Carthage’s Central Park.Globe | Laurie Sisk

Monique Zamarripa and Jo Hannah Close join other volunteers Wednesday as they prepare an Italian dinner for guests at the Ronald McDonald House. The duo joined a group of 10 volunteers from area McDonald’s restaurants in providing the dinner and helping with housekeeping chores.GLOBE | LAURIE SISK

Krypto, a young terrier mix, peers from his kennel Wednesday at the Joplin Humane Society. Krypto is one of an influx of pups available for adoption at the shelter. The Joplin Humane Society is sponsoring a low-cost vaccine clinic from 6 to 9 p.m. Friday for pet owners. GLOBE | LAURIE SISK

Bright Futures Joplin accepted an $8,880 donation from the Million Meals initiative at Arvest Bank to help fund weekend snack packs for food-insecure students at a check presentation ceremony last month. The snack pack program at Bright Futures Joplin feeds about 400 students each weekend wi…

Freedom of Flight Museum President Darryl Coit stands near the cockpit of of T-38 fighter jet simulator Saturday at the museum. The museum offers tours from 2 to 5 p.m. Thursdays and Saturdays. Anyone interested in volunteering may call 417-553-8130. GLOBE | LAURIE SISK

Ivy Simpson (left), 5, and Gabriella Randall, 9, enjoy a dip in Shoal Creek as they beat the heat Saturday at McIndoe Park. Gabriella is a freedom kid, celebrating her 10th birthday on the Fourth of July. And speaking of heat and Monday, the forecast calls for a high in the upper 90s. GLOBE …
